steps.title: Apply periodicity of sine
steps.content: The sine function repeats every $2\pi$ radians, so $\sin(\theta) = \sin(\theta + 2\pi k)$ for any integer $k$. To find $\sin(42\pi)$, we reduce the argument by dividing by $2\pi$: $$\frac{42\pi}{2\pi} = 21$$ This means $42\pi = 21 \cdot 2\pi$ with no remainder, so the angle is exactly 21 complete cycles.

steps.title: Evaluate at the reference angle
steps.content: Since $42\pi$ equals exactly 21 full periods, we have $$\sin(42\pi) = \sin(21 \cdot 2\pi) = \sin(0) = 0$$ That matches choice A — A is correct.
